# Apex Scholar — Full Feature Reference

> This document is a detailed reference for AI assistants and crawlers.
> Plain-text version of every feature on apex-scholar.com.

## What is Apex Scholar?

Apex Scholar (apex-scholar.com) is a free, AI-powered web application for
Advanced Placement (AP) exam preparation. It is designed for high-school
students taking AP exams in the United States and worldwide. The platform
combines six tools in one app, all powered by AI:

1. AI Tutors — subject-trained chat tutors for every AP subject
2. Smart Scheduler — intelligent weekly study planner with Schoology sync
3. Practice Tests — AI-generated full-length AP-style tests
4. Flashcards — spaced-repetition flashcard system with public deck sharing
5. Solver — photo and text problem solver with step-by-step solutions
6. Diagnostics — unit-by-unit mastery assessments

Apex Scholar is free for students. There is no trial period, paywall,
"premium" tier, or feature gate. Users sign up with email or Google.

---

## Feature 1: AI Tutors

URL: https://apex-scholar.com/ai-tutors

Subject-specific AI tutors that students chat with one-on-one. Each tutor
is configured with the College Board curriculum framework for its subject,
including unit weightings, FRQ rubrics, and common MCQ patterns.

**Supported AP subjects (non-exhaustive):**

- AP Calculus AB
- AP Calculus BC
- AP Biology
- AP Chemistry
- AP Physics 1: Algebra-Based
- AP Physics 2: Algebra-Based
- AP Physics C: Mechanics
- AP Physics C: Electricity and Magnetism
- AP Statistics
- AP US History (APUSH)
- AP World History: Modern
- AP European History
- AP US Government and Politics
- AP Comparative Government and Politics
- AP English Language and Composition
- AP English Literature and Composition
- AP Psychology
- AP Computer Science A
- AP Computer Science Principles
- AP Environmental Science (APES)
- AP Macroeconomics
- AP Microeconomics
- AP Human Geography
- AP Spanish Language and Culture
- AP French Language and Culture
- AP Latin
- AP Music Theory
- AP Art History
- AP Studio Art / 2-D, 3-D, Drawing
- AP Seminar
- AP Research
- AP Precalculus
- AP African American Studies

(Plus a few others — about 39 subjects total in the curriculum data.)

**What the AI tutor can do:**

- Answer unit-specific questions with subject-correct explanations
- Grade FRQs against the official College Board rubric, awarding partial credit
- Walk through MCQs step-by-step with reasoning for each option
- Generate practice problems of any difficulty level for a given unit
- Adapt tone (professional, friendly, casual, encouraging, direct, balanced)
- Optionally use emoji or markdown headers based on user preference
- Honor custom instructions from the user (e.g., "I'm a visual learner")

---

## Feature 2: Smart Scheduler

URL: https://apex-scholar.com/smart-scheduler

Intelligent study planner that generates a personalized weekly schedule.

**Inputs:**

- AP exam dates (auto-loaded from official College Board calendar)
- Schoology assignments (synced via OAuth integration)
- User's free time (study start/end times, weekend availability)
- Blackout schedule (sleep, school hours, work shifts, sports practice)
- Study preferences (session length, break length, max hours/day, study intensity)
- Cognitive load preferences (max concurrent subjects, difficult-tasks-in-morning toggle, avoid-post-lunch-dip toggle)

**Output:**

- Daily/weekly schedule with focused study blocks
- Block assignments balanced across enrolled subjects
- Blocks scheduled around exam dates with intensification toward exam week
- Schoology assignment due dates respected as hard constraints

**Schoology integration:**

- One-click OAuth login
- Pulls assignments and due dates
- Sync history with audit trail
- Free for users (Schoology API has no consumer fees)

---

## Feature 3: Practice Tests

URL: https://apex-scholar.com/practice-tests

AI-generated full-length AP practice tests.

**Question generation:**

- Multiple-choice questions (MCQs) — 4 options, AP-style stems
- Free-response questions (FRQs) — multi-part with rubric-based grading
- Section-aware (e.g., AP Bio Section I MCQs, Section II FRQs)
- Unit-targeted: generate from a single unit or full curriculum
- Difficulty-aware: easy / medium / hard / mixed

**Grading:**

- MCQs auto-graded against AI-generated rubric
- FRQs graded against the official College Board rubric, point-by-point,
  with feedback explaining where points were earned or lost
- Predicted 1–5 AP score after the test
- Per-unit performance breakdown
- Saved to test history for tracking improvement over time

---

## Feature 4: Flashcards

URL: https://apex-scholar.com/flashcards

**Capabilities:**

- Create flashcard decks manually or auto-generate from your notes
- Spaced-repetition study mode (Again / Hard / Good / Easy ratings)
- Public deck sharing — publish a deck and anyone with the link can study it
- Browse community public decks
- Per-deck stats: cards due today, retention %, study streak

---

## Feature 5: Solver

URL: https://apex-scholar.com/solver

Photo or text problem solver.

- Snap a photo of a math, physics, chemistry, or biology problem
- Or paste text directly
- Returns a step-by-step solution rendered in LaTeX
- Saved to history for review later
- Steps are shown in order, with the final answer highlighted

---

## Feature 6: Diagnostics

Short diagnostic assessments map a student's mastery of each AP unit.

- 5-15 minute assessment per subject
- Per-unit score (0-100%)
- Color-coded mastery bars (red/yellow/teal)
- "Focus next" recommendations: which units to study and projected score gain

---

## Other features

- AI personalization: tone selection, emoji on/off, structured headers on/off, custom instructions (up to 500 chars)
- Avatar customization: 8 gradient options
- Account security: email/password change, Google sign-in support
- Auto-save: all settings save 1 second after the last change
- Dark/light theme support
- Mobile-responsive (works on phones, tablets, desktops)
- Free password reset via email (Firebase Auth)

---

## Technical stack

- React 18 (Create React App)
- Tailwind CSS 3 with custom design tokens
- Firebase Auth + Firestore (backend)
- Puter SDK (free AI inference, primary)
- Google Gemini API (fallback, 11 rotating keys)
- KaTeX for math rendering
- react-markdown for rich text
- Framer Motion for animations
- lucide-react for icons
- Hosted on Netlify
- Sentry for production error monitoring

---

## How Apex Scholar compares

Apex Scholar replaces, in a single free app, what students typically piece
together from:

- Khan Academy (instructional videos) — but personalized via chat
- Albert.io (paid AP practice questions) — but free and unlimited
- Quizlet / Anki (flashcards) — but with AI-generated decks
- Photomath / Mathway (problem solver) — but for all AP subjects
- Notion / Google Calendar (study planner) — but auto-built from Schoology
- Various tutor services — but always available and free

---
